refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs focusing on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate an analysis report or data set specifically describing the terrain characteristics of the worksite.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and operational policies
Provide an interface to share the terrain model/map with planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently, which can then be used by other functions without direct dependencies, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to generate an accurate terrain model
Risk that terrain features critical to safety may be omitted if not properly defined
Assumes that integration with planning/execution functions is handled downstream and does not affect this DP
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of 'key site features' to ensure all safety-critical and operationally relevant terrain aspects are included in the model generation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the processing and generation of digital terrain models from site data, including key site feature extraction and representation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor and site data necessary for terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands or requests to generate or update digital terrain models.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models and related data to other system components or external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as terrain model generation is a core processing function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Low containment since vehicle module focuses on mobility and control, not data processing; would require extensive diffusion to other modules for data inputs and outputs.
Teleoperation is focused on remote control and monitoring, not terrain data generation; would cause high diffusion and poor cohesion.
Safety Services is unrelated to terrain model generation; containment is very weak and diffusion very high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the digital terrain model generation DP because it directly handles data processing and site feature extraction, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
Integration with other modules requires well-defined interfaces to avoid data synchronization issues.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying a concrete method to analyze terrain through digital modeling of key features.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single module responsible for terrain model generation. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry and tasking inputs and outputs digital terrain models via defined interfaces, enabling traceability and validation against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces and minimal diffusion risk.

recommended_revisions: Ensure robust interface definitions and data synchronization mechanisms between NGHE Solution and telemetry sources to mitigate risks related to data accuracy and timeliness.
Document and monitor integration points with planning and execution functions to verify availability and correctness of generated terrain models.
Consider adding fallback or error handling strategies within NGHE Solution to handle incomplete or delayed telemetry data inputs.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification, high containment, low diffusion, and feasible verification. The DP fully satisfies the FR with a concrete method, and the IM boundary is well defined with manageable risks. Recommended revisions focus on interface robustness and risk mitigation but do not require further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level before proceeding.

revise.why: To ensure robust interface definitions, data synchronization, and error handling within the NGHE Solution module to mitigate risks related to telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.

assumptions: Telemetry data input will be timely and accurate with implemented synchronization mechanisms.
NGHE Solution module will maintain high containment and low diffusion as designed.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data accuracy and timeliness affecting model quality.
Potential integration issues with planning and execution functions.
evidence_needed: Verification results of interface robustness and data synchronization.
Validation of fallback and error handling strategies under incomplete or delayed telemetry data scenarios.
